title: podman-auto-update service starts on system boot
body:
### Issue Description

The `podman-auto-update` service runs on system boot. This is not documented in the man page, hence I believe that this is not by intention. Also running the auto update service concurrently to container/pod startup can lead to races and doesn't seem to be very sensible behavior.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a container/pod and generate a systemd service for it
2. Reboot the machine
3. Observe that the auto update service was started during boot using `sudo journalctl --boot --unit podman-auto-update`


### Describe the results you received

The auto update service does start during system boot

### Describe the results you expected

The auto update service does not start during system boot

### podman info output

```yaml
No relevance.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Did you enabled the `podman-auto-update.service`? This unit should not enabled only the timer should be which runs once a day.

It gets enabled automatically since the [unit](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4bd6bc9f3ccac4d3bd79c4a3eb8fb09e05604a06/contrib/systemd/system/podman-auto-update.service.in#L12-L13) contains an `[Install]` section.

My current workaround is to deploy a systemd preset (`/etc/systemd/system-preset/50-podman-auto-update.preset`) with the following content:

```
disable podman-auto-update.service
```

> It gets enabled automatically since the [unit](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4bd6bc9f3ccac4d3bd79c4a3eb8fb09e05604a06/contrib/systemd/system/podman-auto-update.service.in#L12-L13) contains an `[Install]` section.

That is not how this works, this section means it *can* be enabled not that is *must* be enabled.

IMO enabling the service and timer by default as package is not a good idea and should not be done. You did not provide the info from our template so I have no idea distro/package you are using but it is not done on fedora for example.

So this is not a upstream issue.

Thanks @Luap99 , filed a [distro bug report](https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1095221)

---

Still, I fail to see the use case for an `[Install]` section if a unit is not meant to be run as a service in the first place. YMMV.

I don't know why it was added, likely a misunderstanding. But who knows maybe someone wants to run that on boot somehow and have their other container units ordered to run before this one, even if I doubt that we can never be sure and I see no reason to break anything because of it.
Most importantly if we were to remove it now `systemctl disable podman-auto-update.service` would start to fail so a user could no longer disable it in case it was enabled before the update.
